channel.title: Morning Shift
channel.description: Delivered every Monday by 10 a.m., Weekly Shift examines the latest news in employment, labor and immigration politics and policy.

item.title: DOL turns the page on Chavez-DeRemer
item.description: Acting Labor Secretary Keith Sonderling has filled out his senior team as the agency looks to move past Lori Chavez-DeRemer’s tumultuous tenure.
item.dc:creator: By Nick Niedzwiadek

item.title: Registered Apprenticeships yet to take off
item.description: The Trump administration is making a big bet on Registered Apprenticeships to address the country's skilled worker shortage. It's yet to pay off.
item.dc:creator: By Nick Niedzwiadek

item.title: Lucas faces pressure on religious discrimination
item.description: Equal Employment Opportunity Commission Chair Andrea Lucas said she was “committed to religious liberty” for public and private sector employees.
item.dc:creator: By Lawrence Ukenye

item.title: Big business frets NLRB nominee delay
item.description: Business groups are pressing President Donald Trump to fill a key vacancy on the National Labor Relations Board before it misses an opportunity to shift the balance of power in the workplace.
item.dc:creator: By Nick Niedzwiadek

item.title: White House eyes belt-tightening for DOL, NLRB
item.description: The White House wants to cut billions from the Labor Department’s budget, trim thousands of workers from its payrolls and eliminate parts of the agency loathed by conservatives.
item.dc:creator: By Nick Niedzwiadek

item.title: Anti-union advocates cheer setbacks at Wells Fargo
item.description: Right-to-work advocates are cheering recent union setbacks at Wells Fargo as a sign that the Biden-era boom in interest for organized labor is over.
item.dc:creator: By Nick Niedzwiadek

item.title: Trump DOL rekindles joint-employer push
item.description: The Labor Department is reexamining its rules for when to hold businesses liable for wage and overtime violations committed by their franchisees or subcontractors, a policy that could have wide-ranging effects for everything from fast-food restaurants to temp agencies and construction firms.
item.dc:creator: By Nick Niedzwiadek
